lesson.menu_desc: Quantities, data and quantifiers in sustainability reports -- 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 12 -- Numbers That Matter: Quantities, Data, and Criteria
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Carlos Eduardo | Aula 12 -- Numbers That Matter (Quantifiers)

vocab.title: Match each data word to its meaning
defs: the whole number of something, when you add everything
the number of points something gets in a test or audit
what you get at the end of a check or an audit
following the rules and the requirements
how much or how many of something there is
the middle number of a group, the typical value

paras: The Impact checklist has 82 criteria in total. Eleven of them are critical. Last month, my team checked 40 farms in my region. Most of the farms comply with the rules, but a few farms do not have all the documents yet.

Some farms score above 70 percent, and several farms are close to full compliance. None of the farms failed completely. The average score is 78 out of 100. All the critical criteria are checked twice, because they are the most important.

In a report, I use numbers and quantifiers together. I write: 'There are 82 criteria in total.' 'Most properties score above 70 percent.' 'Only a few farms meet all the requirements.' Numbers show the size, and quantifiers show how many: all, most, some, a few, or none.
reading.source: Alumni by Better -- A1 reading, numbers & quantifiers in reports

reading.prompt: What is the main idea of the text?
choices: Carlos explains how to roast coffee beans at home.
Carlos describes the numbers of the checklist with numbers and quantifiers (all, most, some, a few, none).
Carlos writes an email to book a flight to Geneva.

items: The Impact checklist has 82 criteria in total.
The text says: 'The Impact checklist has 82 criteria in total.'
All the farms failed the audit.
The text says: 'None of the farms failed completely.'
Most of the farms comply with the rules.
The text says: 'Most of the farms comply with the rules.'
The average score is 40 out of 100.
The text says: 'The average score is 78 out of 100.'

parts: of the farms failed. The average score is 78

gapfill.text: Quantifiers show how many: all (100%), most (a big part), some (a part), a few / several (a small number), none (0). Use them with plural nouns: most farms, a few criteria, none of the farms. Numbers show the size: 82 criteria, 78 out of 100, above 70 percent.

bank.label: Useful data sentences for a report
items: Most of the farms comply with the rules.
Only a few farms meet all the requirements.
None of the farms failed completely.
The average score is 78 out of 100.
All the critical criteria are checked twice.

lf.text: all = 100%. most = a big part (more than half). some = a part. a few / several = a small number. none = 0. Use a plural noun after them: most farms, a few criteria, none of the farms.

items.situation: Say how many criteria the checklist has, in total.
tips: The checklist has 82 criteria in total.

items.situation: Say that more than half of the farms comply.
tips: Most farms comply with the rules.

items.situation: Say that a small number of farms are missing documents.
tips: A few farms do not have all the documents.
Only a few farms are missing documents.

items.situation: Say the average score, out of 100.
tips: The average score is 78 out of 100.
On average, the farms score 78 out of 100.

items: Read the totals aloud: 82 criteria, 40 farms, 11 critical points.
Describe the farms: most comply, a few are missing documents, none failed.
Say the scores: some farms are above 70 percent, the average is 78 out of 100.
The auditor asks: 'How many farms comply?' Answer with a quantifier and a number.

answerkey.title: Reveal the data phrases
list: Totals: 'There are 82 criteria in total. Eleven of them are critical.'
Quantifiers: 'Most of the farms comply. A few farms are missing documents. None failed.'
Scores: 'Some farms are above 70 percent. The average score is 78 out of 100.'
answerkey.note: Numbers show the size (82, 78 out of 100). Quantifiers show how many (all, most, some, a few, none).

listenings.text: Let me read the numbers of my checklist. The Impact checklist has eighty-two criteria in total. Eleven of them are critical. Last month, my team checked forty farms in my region. Most of the farms comply with the rules. A few farms do not have all the documents yet. None of the farms failed completely. Some farms score above seventy percent, and several farms are close to full compliance. The average score is seventy-eight out of one hundred. These numbers help me write a clear report for the team in Geneva.

Hi Carlos, this is Emma with two questions about the data. First, how many criteria are critical? I remember it is a small number. Second, do most of the farms comply, or only a few? I want the average score for the report, out of one hundred. Please send me the total number of farms too. If none of them failed, that is very good news. Thank you, Carlos. Your numbers are always clear.

extra_audio.text: First, Carlos opens the Impact checklist. There are eighty-two criteria in total. Eleven of them are critical. Most of the farms comply with the rules. Only a few farms are missing documents. The average score is seventy-eight out of one hundred.
